text: Dear Mr. Chairman, dear colleagues, let us present the case of robot assisted left hepatectomy for cystadenoma adhered to the Keval Hylam. The patient was female of sixty-nine years old with complaints of weak pain in the abdomen upper quadrants during physical activity. That complaints existed for over seven years. Liver cyst was revealed seven years before surgery. Over seven years, the cystic lesion enlarged almost twice, up to ninety-three millimeters.

Open polycystectomy was performed in nineteen eighty five. CT data. Single cystic lesion of ninety three millimeters revealed in left lobe of the liver with complete substitution of the segment four A and prominent compression of segments two, three and four B. Cyst compressed middle and left hepatic vein up to their junction. Mass effect with dilatation of segments two and three bile ducts was presented. Blood level of Tumor Marker C was normal.

The robot-assisted left hepatectomy procedure is shown with two robotic trocars positioned in the lateral quadrants, an optical port near the umbilicus, and three assistant trocars between the robotic and optical ports. The specimen is removed through a 5 cm Pfannenstiel incision.

Two robotic arms equipped with monopolar scissors and a bipolar dissector are used to detach the duodenum from the liver. The cystic serous fluid is aspirated, and a portion of the cystic wall is examined, confirming a benign cystic tumor. The left hepatic artery is isolated, ligated, and transected, followed by the division of the middle hepatic artery. The left portal vein is isolated and ligated without transection. Ischemic demarcation is visible, and the resection line is marked. Segment IV A is completely substituted by the cyst, and the liver parenchyma is transected using robotic scissors.

The surgeon uses bipolar dissector and scissors to transect the liver parenchyma. The branches of the middle hepatic vein are clipped and divided. The left hepatic duct is encircled, ligated, and divided. Branches to segment IV are clipped and divided separately.

The surgeon encircles and transects the left lobe glissonian pedicle. A non-pedicle to the left lobe is also completely transected. Tributaries to the middle hepatic vein are clipped and divided, with bleeding from small branches controlled using bipolar coagulation.

The surgeon carefully detaches the cystic wall from the trunk of the middle hepatic vein, releasing the confluence of the middle hepatic vein and the hepatic vein from segment IV from adhesions with the cystic wall. The hepatic vein from segment IV is encircled, ligated, and divided.

The surgeon ligates and transects the trunk of the left hepatic vein, completing the left hepatectomy. The resection surface reveals the trunk of the middle hepatic vein. A macroscopic view of the specimen is displayed.

Microscopic examination with two hundred and four hundred magnification revealed ovarial tissue in the depth of the cystic wall. Uneventful post-operative course. Patient was discharged after ten days. Thank you for your kind attention.
